user_message: Just got this from Karen Patel at VanArsdel Ltd -- I need help figuring out QBR preparation.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 12:32 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: VanArsdel Ltd QBR preparation

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 17:53 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for VanArsdel Ltd: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-C trap (Re-qualification questions presumptuous); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-C failure mode (Re-qualification questions presumptuous)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-C -- Re-qualification questions presumptuous
